sources.accessNotes: If blocked by region or bot protection, keep URL and verify via browser or official mirror before changing support conclusions.
verificationRules: Do not use this alone to prove AWS Bedrock feature availability.
Pair with AWS Bedrock model docs or model card for hosted support claims.

sources.accessNotes: If blocked by region or bot protection, keep URL and verify via browser or official mirror before changing support conclusions.
verificationRules: Do not use this alone to prove Vertex AI feature availability.
Pair with Google Vertex AI Claude partner docs for hosted support claims.

sources.accessNotes: AWS docs may split details across supported models, model parameters, and model provider pages.
verificationRules: Use AWS docs as authoritative for Bedrock regions, APIs, quotas, model IDs, and hosted feature availability.
Check linked Anthropic model pages or cards when capability details are not on the supported-models page.

sources.surface: Amazon Bedrock Anthropic Claude inference parameters
sources.accessNotes: Validate against current model-specific notes before answering about newly released features.
verificationRules: Use for Bedrock request and response behavior, not for OpenAI/Azure/Gemini claims.
If a parameter is absent here, mark support unknown unless another AWS official page confirms it.

sources.surface: Amazon Bedrock model provider pages and model cards
useFor: AWS-hosted Anthropic model card entry points
sources.accessNotes: Product pages are useful entry points but must be backed by AWS documentation for exact support details.
verificationRules: Do not rely on marketing pages alone for API capability conclusions.
Follow links to AWS documentation or console model cards when available.

sources.accessNotes: Google Cloud docs are authoritative for Vertex regions, endpoints, IAM, quotas, and hosted partner-model behavior.
verificationRules: Use for Vertex AI Claude support claims rather than Anthropic first-party API docs.
Check linked partner model pages for model-specific limitations.

sources.accessNotes: Use as context; exact Claude claims should still cite Claude-specific Vertex pages.
verificationRules: Use overview only for general partner model concepts.
For features, find the model-family page or mark unknown.

sources.accessNotes: Azure docs are authoritative for Azure-hosted GPT/OpenAI model availability and versioning.
verificationRules: Do not infer Azure availability from OpenAI first-party launch notes.
Check region and deployment constraints before saying supported.

sources.accessNotes: Foundry model catalog and Azure OpenAI are related but not identical surfaces; confirm which service the user means.
verificationRules: Separate Foundry catalog availability from Azure OpenAI API support.
Use service-specific docs for endpoints, auth, and feature claims.

sources.accessNotes: Azure API versions gate features; always cite the API version when relevant.
verificationRules: Check API version before claiming support for parameters or endpoints.
Do not treat OpenAI API reference as interchangeable with Azure OpenAI reference.

sources.accessNotes: Use only for OpenAI first-party API unless cross-provider docs explicitly confirm parity.
verificationRules: Do not use to prove Azure OpenAI support.
Pair with OpenAI API docs for endpoint-specific behavior.

sources.accessNotes: OpenAI docs may require browser access for dynamic content; preserve URL if fetch fails.
verificationRules: Use for first-party OpenAI API answers only unless comparing differences.
If Azure has a matching-looking parameter, still verify in Azure docs.

sources.accessNotes: Use Google Cloud docs as authoritative for Vertex AI endpoint, region, IAM, and enterprise feature behavior.
verificationRules: Do not infer Vertex AI support from Gemini API docs without Google Cloud confirmation.
Check region, model version, and API surface before answering.

sources.accessNotes: API reference is the preferred source for endpoint and parameter support.
verificationRules: Use for Vertex AI only; Gemini Developer API has separate docs.
If a feature appears in Gemini API but not Vertex reference, mark Vertex support unknown or provider-specific.

sources.accessNotes: Use for Gemini API direct usage, not Vertex AI enterprise-hosted claims unless Google docs explicitly state parity.
verificationRules: Do not use to prove Vertex AI support.
Pair with Gemini API reference for endpoint-specific details.

sources.accessNotes: Model names and feature support change quickly; perform live check for current production answers.
verificationRules: Use for Gemini API only unless Vertex docs explicitly confirm the same model and feature.
For availability or billing in Google Cloud, use Vertex AI docs instead.
